item.title: Problem: ATM Withdrawal Simulator (Thailand Edition)
item.author: Rizwanul Islam Rudra
item.content:encoded: You are simulating the behavior of an ATM machine in Thailand. The ATM can dispense only three denominations of Thai Baht (THB): 100, 500, and 1000.When a user enters a withdrawal amount, the ATM will always prioritize the largest denomination first, working downwards. Your task is to determine which banknotes are dispensed.The ATM has the following rules:It always dispenses the least number of banknotes possible, starting from the highest denomination.The maximum withdrawal per transaction is 50,000 THB. If the user requests more than this amount, the system should return an error message.If the amount is not exactly divisible by the available denominations (e.g., 125), the system should return an error stating the amount cannot be dispensed.def withdraw(amount: int) -> Union[List[int], str]: (): The amount of Thai Baht the user wants to withdraw.Constraint: A list of integers representing the denominations dispensed (e.g., )Or a string error message in the following cases:If the amount exceeds 50,000 THB.If the amount cannot be dispensed using only 100, 500, and 1000 THB notes.Input: 3500 Output: [1000, 1000, 1000, 500] Input: 370 Output: "Cannot dispense the requested amount with available denominations." Input: 60000 Output: "Withdrawal amount exceeds the maximum limit of 50,000 THB." The ATM only supports denominations: 100, 500, 1000 THB.All inputs are positive integers.Return the minimum number of notes required to fulfill the amount.Return appropriate error messages if the request is invalid.Try to solve the problem with Time and Space ComplexityTry solving the challenge and feel free to share your solution in the comments below. I'd love to see how you approach it — whether it's in Python, JavaScript, or any other language!

item.title: 🏯Beginner-Friendly Guide "Find Lucky Integer in an Array" – LeetCode 1394 (C++ | Python | JavaScript)
item.author: Om Shree
item.content:encoded: Today we’re tackling a charming problem that blends frequency counting and a bit of luck. The idea? Identify integers in an array whose value matches their count. These are called — and our goal is to find the largest one!Let's dive into the logic and crack the problem in all three major languages. 🎯Return the largest in the array. An integer is considered if its frequency in the array equals its own value.If no such integer exists, return .Count the frequency of each number in the array.Check which numbers have frequency equal to their value.Return the such number.Since values are capped at 500, we can use a frequency array for efficient counting.Use frequency maps or arrays to track counts.Look for numbers where .Always return the largest such number (or if none).This problem is a great exercise in hash maps and frequency counting. Simple, clean, and a great fit for coding interviews or warmups.Keep the code flowing, and see you in the next breakdown! 🚀

item.title: MLOps: Bridging the Gap Between Machine Learning Models and Real-World Applications
item.author: Dev Patel
item.content:encoded: Imagine you've baked the most delicious cake ever – a complex masterpiece of flavor and texture. But it sits on your kitchen counter, admired only by you. To share this culinary triumph, you need a system to bake it consistently, package it beautifully, and deliver it to eager customers. This is essentially what MLOps does for machine learning models.MLOps, a portmanteau of "Machine Learning Operations," is the set of practices that aims to streamline the entire lifecycle of a machine learning model, from its initial development to its deployment and ongoing maintenance. It's the bridge between the data scientists who build these powerful models and the engineers who ensure they function reliably in real-world applications. Without MLOps, the potential of machine learning often remains unrealized, trapped in the research lab or stuck in a buggy, inefficient state.Understanding the Core Concepts:Think of a typical software development lifecycle. You write code, test it, deploy it, and then monitor its performance, making updates as needed. MLOps applies a similar structured approach to machine learning, focusing on several key areas:Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery (CI/CD): Just like in software engineering, CI/CD in MLOps automates the process of building, testing, and deploying machine learning models. This ensures consistent quality and speeds up the deployment process, allowing for rapid iteration and improvement. Every change to the model, the code, or the data is tracked meticulously. This allows for easy rollback to previous versions if something goes wrong, and it also facilitates collaboration among team members. Think of it like keeping detailed recipes for your cake – you can always go back and tweak a previous version if needed. Data scientists often experiment with different models, algorithms, and hyperparameters. MLOps provides tools to systematically track these experiments, comparing their performance and making informed decisions about which model to deploy. This is like keeping a detailed log of your cake baking experiments, noting down ingredients, baking times, and feedback.Model Monitoring and Management: Once a model is deployed, it's crucial to monitor its performance continuously. Data drifts, unexpected inputs, or simply changing patterns in the environment can degrade a model's accuracy over time. MLOps provides the tools to detect and address these issues, ensuring the model remains effective. This is like regularly checking your cake's quality, ensuring it remains fresh and appealing.Infrastructure Management: Training and deploying machine learning models often requires significant computational resources. MLOps helps manage this infrastructure efficiently, leveraging cloud platforms or on-premise solutions to optimize resource utilization and cost. This is analogous to having the right kitchen equipment and space to bake your cake efficiently.The Significance of MLOps:MLOps addresses several critical challenges in the machine learning world: Ensuring that a model's performance can be replicated consistently. Deploying models to handle large volumes of data and users. Facilitating efficient teamwork between data scientists, engineers, and other stakeholders. Keeping models up-to-date and performing optimally over time. Reducing the time it takes to get a model from development to production.MLOps is transforming various industries: Fraud detection, risk assessment, algorithmic trading. Disease prediction, personalized medicine, drug discovery. Recommendation systems, inventory management, personalized marketing. Predictive maintenance, quality control, supply chain optimization.Challenges and Ethical Considerations:While MLOps offers significant advantages, it also presents some challenges: Implementing MLOps can be complex, requiring specialized skills and tools. Setting up and maintaining the necessary infrastructure can be expensive.Data Security and Privacy: MLOps systems must be designed to protect sensitive data. MLOps practices need to incorporate mechanisms to detect and mitigate bias in machine learning models. Ensuring fairness and ethical considerations are paramount.MLOps is still a relatively young field, but its importance is undeniable. As machine learning becomes more pervasive, the need for robust and efficient MLOps practices will only grow. We can expect to see further advancements in automation, improved tooling, and a greater emphasis on ethical considerations. The ultimate goal is to make machine learning more accessible, reliable, and beneficial to society as a whole. Just like our perfectly baked cake, the potential of MLOps is vast, and with proper implementation, it can deliver delicious results across various sectors.

item.title: Thread Smart, Process Hard: Mastering Python's Parallel Playbook
item.author: Chandrani Mukherjee
item.content:encoded:
Python is often accused of being “slow.” While it’s true that Python isn’t as fast as C or Rust in raw computation, with the right techniques, you can significantly speed up your Python code—especially if you're dealing with I/O-heavy workloads.In this post, we’ll dive into:When and how to use in Python.How it differs from .How to identify and workloads.Practical examples that can boost your app’s performance.
🧠 Understanding I/O-Bound vs CPU-Bound Before choosing between threading or multiprocessing, you must understand the type of task you're optimizing:Spends most time waiting for external resourcesWeb scraping, File downloadsSpends most time performing heavy computationsImage processing, ML inference: If your program is slow because it's , use . If it's slow because it's , use .
🧵 Using Threading in Python Python’s Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) limits true parallelism for CPU-bound threads, but for , can bring a huge speed boost.
Example: Threading for I/O-bound Tasks 🕒 Without threads, this would take ~6 seconds (2s per request). With threads, it runs in ~2 seconds, showing real speedup.💡 Threading Caveats Threads share memory → race conditions possible.Use threading.Lock() to avoid shared resource conflicts.Ideal for I/O, but not effective for CPU-heavy work.
🧮 Multiprocessing for CPU-Bound Tasks For CPU-heavy workloads, the GIL becomes a bottleneck. That’s where the multiprocessing module comes in. It spawns separate processes, each with its own Python interpreter.Example: CPU-bound Task with MultiprocessingHere, we divide the work across all available CPU cores — a massive boost for computationally expensive tasks.
🔍 How to Tell if a Task is CPU-Bound or I/O-Bound Use profiling tools or observation:Visual inspection Waiting on API calls, file reads → I/O-boundMath loops, data crunching → CPU-boundpip line_profiler kernprof script.py python line_profiler script.py.lprof python cProfile myscript.py Check where is spent: I/O calls or computation. 🧰 Bonus: concurrent.futures for Clean Code Instead of manually managing threads or processes, use:✅ Final Thoughts Python isn’t inherently slow—it just needs the right tools.Task Type Use This I/O-bound threading, asyncio, ThreadPoolExecutor CPU-bound multiprocessing, ProcessPoolExecutorStart small, profile your code, and choose the right parallelization strategy. Your app—and your users—will thank you.

item.title: # Introducing PrettyParser: Your Go-To Tool for Code Beautification and Minifica
item.author: Helpothon
item.content:encoded:
In the fast-paced world of software development, clarity and efficiency are paramount. Whether you are working on a large-scale application or a simple web page, maintaining clean code structure not only enhances readability but also optimizes performance. This is where PrettyParser steps in—a versatile code beautifier and minifier designed specifically for JSON, HTML, and XML. Let’s dive into its features and see how it can elevate your coding experience.
Key Features of PrettyParser One of the standout features of PrettyParser is its ability to transform messy, unformatted code into a well-structured format. This is especially useful for JSON, HTML, and XML files, which can become difficult to read when not properly formatted. With just a click, you can turn a long string of code into neatly indented blocks, making it easier to visualize the structure of your data.On the flip side, when you’re ready to deploy your application, minimizing file size becomes crucial for performance. PrettyParser allows you to minify your code efficiently. It removes unnecessary whitespace, comments, and other extraneous elements without affecting functionality. This ensures that your web applications load faster and use fewer resources, which is crucial for an optimal user experience.
JSON, HTML, and XML Support PrettyParser is built around the common formats developers use daily. Whether you're handling complex JSON data structures, crafting intricate HTML layouts, or managing XML configuration files, PrettyParser has you covered. The tool ensures seamless handling of these formats while maintaining data integrity throughout the beautification and minification processes.In today’s privacy-focused environment, data security is a primary concern for many developers. PrettyParser addresses this by ensuring that no data is stored. All operations are performed in real-time, safeguarding your code and maintaining your privacy. You can use the tool with the peace of mind that your data is not being collected or stored anywhere.PrettyParser has been designed with developers in mind. Its straightforward interface makes it easy to navigate, allowing you to focus on your code rather than getting bogged down by complex settings. Whether you are a seasoned developer or just starting your coding journey, you'll find PrettyParser intuitive and efficient.Getting started with PrettyParser is easy. Simply visit PrettyParser and follow the straightforward prompts to beautify or minify your code. The tool is designed to be user-friendly, so you can jump right in without any steep learning curves.In a world where time is of the essence and clarity is key, PrettyParser stands out as an essential tool for any developer's toolkit. With its powerful features for code beautification and minification, along with support for JSON, HTML, and XML, PrettyParser helps you produce clean, efficient, and optimized code.If you are looking to enhance your coding workflow, consider giving PrettyParser a try. Head over to PrettyParser and see how it can make your development process smoother and more efficient.

item.title: Django ORM: consultas complexas
item.author: Lucas Mateus
item.content:encoded: O ORM do Django é simples e intuitivo no seu uso mas muitos devs acreditam que consultas SQL mais avançadas são demais para essa ferramenta.A verdade é que o ORM do Django vai muito além do consultas básicas, e nesta publicação vou mostrar como usar recursos poderosos deste ORM para resolver situações reais de forma limpa e performática.Um dev iniciante com o ORM, provavelmente faria uma operação de atualizar um campo dessa seguinte maneira, o que não só requer mais etapas, além de que esse fluxo pode ser problemático, especialmente em situações de concorrência, porque o valor pode mudar entre o SELECT e o UPDATE.A classe permite referenciar valores de outros campos da mesma linha diretamente no banco, sem precisar carregar os dados pro Python.Com , a operação ocorre direto no banco de dados de forma segura:Essa operação é atômica, ou seja, evita condições de corrida entre processos concorrentes. O Django gera diretamente um UPDATE estoque = estoque - 1 no banco, sem realizar um antes. Sendo esse o código equivalente em .Como já estamos acostumados a fazer utlizando SQL, são ideais quando você precisa anexar dados derivados de outras tabelas. Vejamos essa consulta feita em SQL puro que anexa o último pedido de um cliente a consulta.A primeiro momento parece bem chato fazer essa usando somente o ORM, mas vamos ver algumas classes que irão nos ajudar com isso. e são usados juntos para inserir subconsultas SQL dentro de uma consulta principal. O permite definir uma consulta que será executada para cada linha da consulta externa. Já o é usado dentro da subconsulta para referenciar dinamicamente uma coluna da consulta externa (como o ID do cliente no nosso caso). Essa mesma consulta agora feita somente com o Django ORM ficaria assim: Sempre use nas para garantir que a subconsulta retorne exatamente um valor escalar, evitando erros e tornando a consulta válida para ser usada em .Em SQL, window functions (ou funções de janela) são funções que realizam cálculos em um conjunto de linhas relacionando à linha atual sem colapsar o resultado como fazem as funções de agregação tradicionais (, , , etc). Queries com são úteis para análises que dependem de agrupamentos e ordenações, e o Django já suporta isso de forma nativa.O Django ORM vai muito além do que parece. Ferramentas como as vistas nesta publicação permitem expressar consultas complexas com clareza e segurança, direto do Python. Em vez de fugir pro SQL cru de cara, tente explorar esses recursos. Muitas vezes, a solução está ao seu alcance e pode deixar o seu código bem mais limpo do que você imagina. Existem ainda mais funções complexas do ORM a serem exploradas, quem sabe não exploro elas numa próxima publicação, até a próxima.Você pode dar uma olhada com mais calma nessa e outras várias funções avançadas de consulta, diretamente na documentação do Django:

item.title: Complete Guide: Using AlpacaTradingAgent Web UI for Automated Trading
item.author: AaronTrng
item.content:encoded:
: This system is designed for research and educational purposes. Always start with paper trading mode before risking real money.🆕 What’s New — AlpacaTradingAgent🔑 Getting Started: API Keys Setup📈 Picking Symbols to Analyze📈 Understanding Investment vs Trading Modes⏰ Scheduling Analysis and Trading🧠 Choosing Your LLMs — Quick vs Deep Thinker👀 Monitoring Your Operations💼 Managing Positions and Orders📊 Reading Reports and Results🧩 How the Multi-Agent Brain Works🆘 Troubleshooting Common IssuesModern, reactive dashboard built with Plotly Dash – live charts, tabbed agent reports, manual order buttons, no CLI needed.Five-agent brain (was four)• (technicals)• (headline sentiment)• (Reddit/Twitter pulse)• (financials & comps)• (FRED data: Fed speeches, CPI, GDP, rates)LLM prompts rewritten around End-of-Day decision-making – less noise from intraday whipsaws, tighter alignment with closing prices.Debate & 3-layer Risk GateBull vs Bear researchers debate, then three independent Risk-Manager agents vet position size, stop, and exposure before any order routes to Alpaca.Analyse and trade , , , , etc. in one batch (crypto data via CoinDesk & DeFi Llama).Multi-symbol parallel runsJust type – the stack spins up all agents for each symbol concurrently.• Continuous loop every hours (ideal for 24/7 crypto)• Market-hour triggers for equities (e.g. run at 10 AM & 3 PM ET).Full support for paper or live accounts, fractional shares, and margin long/short.Real-time buying power, cash, open P/L, and for any position.
🔑 Getting Started: API Keys Setup Before you can use AlpacaTradingAgent's web UI for automated trading, you'll need to obtain API keys from several financial data providers. This section will guide you through each provider step-by-step.
1. 🏦 Alpaca API Keys (Required for Trading and fetching stock chart) Alpaca is your primary trading broker and the for this system.Steps to get Alpaca API Keys:Complete the account verification process (this may take 1-3 business days)Once verified, log into your Alpaca dashboardNavigate to section in your account settingsClick and give it a descriptive name like Copy both the and - you'll need bothIf you want the agent to be able to perform shorting. You should turn on in the Alpaca Account's .: Keep your API keys secure and never share them publicly!Important Configuration Notes:✅ for risk-free testinghttps://paper-api.alpaca.markets/v2⚠️ to trade real moneyhttps://api.alpaca.markets
2. 🤖 OpenAI API Key (Required for AI Analysis) OpenAI powers the multi-agent analysis system that makes trading decisions.Steps to get OpenAI API Key:Create an account or log in to your existing accountCreate a project and name itNavigate to section in Click Give it a name like Copy the generated key immediately ()We recommend using for both quick and deep thinking models during testing, it has the websearch feature and still minimizes costsThe system makes numerous API calls across Monitor your OpenAI usage dashboard regularly
3. 📰 Finnhub API Key (Required for Stock News) Finnhub provides real-time financial news and market data.Steps to get Finnhub API Key:Verify your email addressLog in and navigate to your dashboardYour API key will be displayed on the main dashboard pageCopy the key for later use
4. 🏛️ FRED API Key (Required for Macro Analysis) FRED (Federal Reserve Economic Data) provides macroeconomic indicators.Steps to get FRED API Key:Fill out the registration form ()Check your section for the Save the key for configuration
5. ₿ CoinDesk API Key (Required for Crypto Analysis) For cryptocurrency news and data analysis.Steps to get CoinDesk API Key:Navigate to sectionCopy the key for your configuration
1️⃣ Clone the Repository
git clone https://github.com/huygiatrng/AlpacaTradingAgent.git AlpacaTradingAgent Keep the project inside its own folder so the relative paths in the web UI (logs, checkpoints, screenshots) work out-of-the-box.
2️⃣ Create & Activate a Python Environment
conda create alpacatradingagent 3.13 conda activate alpacatradingagent If you prefer /, that works too—just make sure it’s .
3️⃣ Install Dependencies
pip requirements.txt
pip pip You should see all packages install without errors; the heavy ones are , , , and the OpenAI client.
4️⃣ Configure Your Secrets and drop in your keys:Keep this file —never commit it to GitHub.: Never commit your file to version control or share it publicly!
🚀 Starting the Application Launch the web interface using:
🖥️ Accessing the Interface Navigate to You'll see the top with 2 panels which show your current positions and recent orders, which are fetched from your Alpaca accountBelow are the config, chart, status, and decision panels, which are set at their default settings.And the bottom is the reports panel, where we can navigate analysts' reports, debates, and managers' reports of our agent on multiple symbols.
📈 Picking Symbols to Analyze In AlpacaTradingAgent, you can analyze both and using a simple input format. The system will run a full AI-driven analysis on each symbol you specify.✅ For : just use the (e.g., , , )✅ For : use the format (e.g., , ): Always include for crypto symbols so the agent recognizes them correctly.Bitcoin (crypto) and Tesla (stock)Ethereum (crypto), Apple (stock), and Solana (crypto): Stick to supported formats. Don't use or – always enter , , etc.The system can handle in a single run:NVDA, ETH/USD, GOOGL, BTC/USD Once entered, the agents will begin processing each symbol in parallel and deliver tabbed reports with deep insights per asset.Before starting the analysis, you can choose you want to include in the decision-making process. This allows you to tailor the depth and focus of each run based on your strategy.Performs technical analysis on charts, price patterns, and volume indicatorsExtracts and evaluates sentiment and impact of recent financial newsAnalyzes economic indicators (GDP, interest rates, inflation, etc.) from FREDTracks sentiment trends on Reddit, Twitter, and other platformsReviews company financials, balance sheets, P/E ratio, and earnings reportsTurn for full-spectrum analysisUse for traditional investingUse for short-term sentiment-based tradesUse for positions affected by economic cycles: You can rerun the analysis with different combinations to compare perspectives.Once selected, these analysts will generate their individual reports, and the system will automatically synthesize their insights into a final recommendation.You can customize how deeply the AI agents debate, analyze, and deliberate before making a recommendation. This affects how long the analysis takes and how thorough the results are.Quick research with minimal discussionmax_risk_discuss_rounds = 1Fast results without deep debateBalanced analysis and agent debatemax_risk_discuss_rounds = 3General use cases and regular tradingFull in-depth research with extensive deliberationmax_risk_discuss_rounds = 5High-stakes decisions and deep dives: More depth = more time + more OpenAI usage. Use for quick checks and when precision matters.Select the in the sidebar before clicking The number of and will follow your selected depthEach agent will use the depth to determine how much reasoning to perform before making conclusions
📈 Understanding Investment vs Trading Modes The AlpacaTradingAgent supports to market participation:
💎 Investment Mode (Buy/Hold/Sell) : Long-term focus (days to months): Buy and hold approach: Suitable for retirement accounts and cash accounts: , , or existing positionsBuilding long-term wealthRetirement portfolio management
⚡ Trading Mode (Short/Neutral/Long) : Short-term focus (minutes to days): Active position management: Requires margin account for short selling: (sell/bet against), (no position), (buy/bet for)Day trading and swing tradingHedging existing positionsActive portfolio management: Trading mode requires a margin account and involves higher risk🏦Turn on margin for Alpaca Paper account:You can adjust the Max Margin Multiplier inside the Alpaca Paper account .
⏰ Scheduling Analysis and Trading The web UI provides two powerful scheduling options for automated analysis and trading that you can turn on if you want:
🔄 Loop Scheduling (Continuous Analysis) Input your symbols (e.g., )Navigate to the "Scheduling Configuration " section in the web UISet the (e.g., for 60 minutes after finished previous analysis)Enable/disable auto-execution of trades (switch in section)✅ Runs continuously (suited best for cryptocurrency trading)Start with longer intervals () for testingUse initiallyMonitor API usage costs with frequent analysis
🕐 Scheduled Analysis (Specific Time during Market hours) Absolutely! Here's the completed section for , incorporating the two image states you provided:You can decide whether to manually review analysis results or let the AI automatically place trades based on the final decision.
🖐️ Manual Trading Mode When is turned , the system enters .Analysis results will be shown for your review. No trades will be executed automatically.❌ No automatic orders will be placed👁️ Review analysis results manually🧑‍💻 Execute trades manually through your broker (e.g., Alpaca dashboard) you're testing, learning, or want full control over execution.Turn ON the toggle to let the agent based on its final decision.The system will execute trades using the specified per symbol.✅ Automatically places trades after analysis finishes📏 Uses fractional or integer shares (depending on your Alpaca settings)📈 Executes trades based on strategy and risk assessment💸 Each symbol will be allocated the full amount you set (e.g., \$4500 per symbol)If and is trading at , the system will purchase (not fractional unless enabled in Alpaca).Specify the amount of capital to use . This is applied individually to each symbol being analyzed.No trade (unless fractional trading is on)
🧠 Choosing Your LLMs — Quick vs Deep Thinker Fast, surface-level reasoning, headline scraping, fact-gathering. Runs dozens of short calls per analysis.⚡ Low latency • 🕸️ Built-in web-search ⭐ (cheapest)Long-form synthesis, weighing evidence, writing structured reports. Runs only a handful of heavier calls.🧠 High reasoning depth • 🕸️ Web-search optional 🏆 (best accuracy) family if cost-sensitive the (, , ) can call the built-in browser tool. That makes them ideal for the Quick Thinker, which needs fresh headlines, Reddit threads, etc. Set both slots to . You keep web-search capability at the lowest token price while you shake down the pipeline. Use (Quick) + (Deep). You’ll pay a bit more per run but gain noticeably tighter reasoning in Researcher debates and Risk reviews.You can switch models any time in the dropdowns shown above—changes take effect on the very next analysis cycle.Keep an eye on your OpenAI usage dashboard; the Quick Thinker makes many more calls, so its model choice has the biggest impact on cost.
👀 Monitoring Your Operations
📊 Real-Time Progress Tracking The web UI provides comprehensive monitoring capabilities:
🎛️ Analysis Report Panel Overview
📺 Simulate Debate of Researchers and Risk Managers 🔄 Real-Time Updates Include:🤖 Agent decisions and reasoning📰 News events affecting analyzed symbols⚠️ Risk management alerts✅ Trade execution confirmations: Keep the live feed open to monitor agent decision-making in real-time
💼 Managing Positions and Orders
📈 Current Positions Dashboard Symbol, quantity, market value, unrealised P/LClick to close immediately, or open the symbol to adjust protective stopsEvery submission, fill and cancel (status-coloured)Cancel or duplicate pending orders; click an order row to view the full JSON sent to AlpacaReal-time equity, cash and margin headroomThe panels refresh automatically every few seconds; no manual reload needed.
📊 Reading Reports and Results The web UI organizes analysis results into digestible, tabbed reports: – one-glance recommendation ( or ), conviction score and risk rating. – annotated 1-year candlestick chart with volume, key moving averages and break-out levels. – latest headlines with sentiment scores plus Reddit/Twitter mention heat-maps. – valuation multiples, growth metrics and peer comparison for equities; on-chain and macro drivers for crypto. – suggested position size, stop distance and scenario analysis.
🧩 How the Multi-Agent Brain Works Below is a step-by-step walk-through of the five-layer “think → debate → trade → vet → commit” pipeline illustrated in your schema. Use it as the “how it works” section in your docs.1. Analysts (5 specialists)• – price/volume via Alpaca + Stockstats• – headlines & sentiment (Google News, Finnhub, CoinDesk)• – Reddit pulse + WebSearch• – earnings, comps (Finnhub, SimFin, DeFi Llama)• – FRED macro seriesEach analyst ingests its data silo, extracts features, and produces a JSON “evidence packet” (metrics, charts, sentiment scores, etc.).• • Both researchers read every evidence packet and argue in an internal loop (⁠_N_ rounds set by “Research Depth”⁠).- Bull builds the pro case (why to buy/long)- Bear builds the con case (why to sell/short)All arguments cite the analyst evidence, so nothing is “hand-waved.”Acts as the referee/critic. Scores each argument for quality, flags missing data, and produces an Executive Research Report summarising:• strongest buy points• open questions / confidence levelConverts the Research Report into a :• direction (BUY / SELL / LONG / SHORT / NEUTRAL)• tentative size (shares or USD)• stop-loss & take-profit levels• • • Three independent risk profiles stress-test the proposal (position sizing, VaR, liquidity, correlation). They debate and may scale size up/down or veto trades that breach their limits.Reviews the three risk opinions, the trader’s rationale, and current portfolio exposures. Chooses the : approve, modify, or cancel.If approved, submits orders to Alpaca, watches fills, and feeds real-time status back to the dashboard.
🆘 Troubleshooting Common Issues Agents stuck at “PENDING”Downgrade research depth or raise quotaOrders “REJECTED” by broker on but insufficient buying powerReduce or deposit fundsBoth and untickedClick or refresh browser – default is infinite loop when neither scheduler is activeThe gives you an end-to-end command centre for research-grade analysis one-click execution. After working through this guide you can:Wire up every required API key (Alpaca, OpenAI, Finnhub, FRED, CoinDesk)Live data, AI brains and broker access all in one placeLock down the environment with a clean and an isolated Conda envKeeps secrets safe and dependencies conflict-freeFlip between Investment ↔ Trading modesMatch the agent’s tactics to your time-horizon and risk profile — continuous loops, clock-based slots or bothAutomate everything from hourly crypto scans to 10 AM & 2 PM equity check-insTrack progress in real time with colour-coded status, live fills and P/LNo need to refresh — the dashboard streams updates as they happenManage positions and orders directly from the UILiquidate, scale in/out or tweak stops without leaving the pageDrill into multi-agent reports to see exactly how each specialist reached the callTransparency beats black-box signals every time start in mode with small order sizes. Prove the loop, study the reports, then graduate to live capital when you’re satisfied.The agent stack is powerful, but it’s still a tool — final responsibility rests with risk limits and investment plan.⚠️ Research & Education only. Markets carry risk and past results are not predictive. Do your own due diligence or consult a licensed professional before committing real funds.Happy trading & clear skies! 🚀📈

item.title: How I Structure My FastAPI Projects for Clean, Scalable Code
item.author: Muha-mmed
item.content:encoded: When I started working with FastAPI, I quickly realized one thing: Project structure matters.A messy project becomes hard to scale, hard to maintain, and hard to debug — especially when you’re working on real-world APIs or collaborating with a team.Over time, I settled on a project structure that keeps things organized, clear, and ready to grow. In this post, I’ll walk you through the structure I use and why it works for me.🗂 FastAPI Project Structure ExampleHere’s what my folder structure looks like for a typical FastAPI project:app/ ├── admin/ → Admin operations (separate logic for admins) ├── models/ → SQLAlchemy database models ├── schemas/ → Pydantic request & response schemas ├── crud/ → Database operations (clean, reusable functions) ├── routes/ → API endpoints, cleanly separated by feature ├── core/ → Auth logic, password hashing, reusable dependencies ├── database.py → Database connection & session setup ├── config.py → App settings & environment variables alembic/ → Database migrations (optional, recommended) env/ → Virtual environment folder (optional) .env → Secrets like DB URI, JWT keys requirements.txt → Project dependencies 💡 Why I Like This Structure✔️ Separation of Concerns — Each folder has a clear responsibility ✔️ Easy to Scale — As features grow, everything stays organized ✔️ Cleaner Routes — No messy logic inside route files ✔️ Team Friendly — Easy for new devs to onboard and understand ✔️ Good with Extensions — Works well with JWT auth, Alembic, etc.📦 Quick Notes on Key Folders • models/ — Pure database models using SQLAlchemy • schemas/ — Pydantic schemas for validation and serialization • crud/ — Handles database queries so routes stay clean • routes/ — Your API endpoints organized by feature (e.g., auth.py, coffee.py) • core/ — Authentication logic, password hashing, reusable dependencies like get_current_user • admin/ — Optional, useful for admin-specific operationsA clean project structure won’t solve all your problems — but it definitely saves you time and headaches down the road.This structure works for me on both small and growing FastAPI projects, and I’m always open to improving it.How do you structure your FastAPI apps? I’d love to hear your suggestions, ideas, or different approaches in the comments! 🙌

item.title: # Introducing FormatWeaver: Your Universal File Format Converter
item.author: Helpothon
item.content:encoded:
In the ever-evolving landscape of technology, file formats are often a source of frustration for developers and users alike. With various applications and systems relying on specific formats, the need for a powerful and efficient file conversion tool has never been greater. That’s where FormatWeaver comes into play.FormatWeaver is a universal file format converter designed to transform any file format into another seamlessly. Whether you’re dealing with documents, images, audio, video, or any other file type, FormatWeaver takes the hassle out of conversion. With its browser-based processing, you can convert files without the need for cumbersome software installations or configurations.
Universal File Conversion The core of FormatWeaver’s functionality lies in its ability to handle a wide array of file formats. Gone are the days of juggling multiple conversion tools for different file types. From converting PDFs to Word documents, images to audio files, or even complex formats like CAD files, FormatWeaver can manage it all in one unified platform.With FormatWeaver’s browser-based processing, you can quickly convert files directly from your web browser. This means no more waiting for lengthy downloads or the hassle of installing bulky applications. Simply upload your file, select the desired output format, and let FormatWeaver do the heavy lifting in real-time. This convenience makes it an ideal tool for developers who need quick conversions in their workflow.One of the standout features of FormatWeaver is its commitment to user privacy. Unlike many file conversion services that store your data on their servers, FormatWeaver processes files in a way that ensures no data storage. Your files are converted and discarded immediately after processing, meaning you can use the tool with complete peace of mind, knowing that your data remains confidential and secure.In an age where data privacy is paramount, FormatWeaver puts the security of your information at the forefront. By eliminating data storage and processing files directly in the browser, you avoid the risks associated with uploading sensitive data to external servers. This focus on privacy makes FormatWeaver a reliable choice for developers who need to handle confidential information during file conversions.
Why Developers Should Care As developers, we often find ourselves needing to convert files for various reasons—whether it’s preparing assets for deployment, ensuring compatibility across different systems, or simply streamlining our workflow. FormatWeaver addresses these needs with a robust, user-friendly platform that integrates seamlessly into your existing processes. Imagine handling file conversions as part of your development pipeline without worrying about the complexities of format compatibility. With FormatWeaver, you can focus on what you do best—building and creating, while the tool takes care of the file format headaches.If you’re ready to experience the convenience and efficiency of a universal file format converter, head over to FormatWeaver and try it out for yourself. The future of file conversion is here, and it’s just a click away. Join the community of developers embracing this innovative tool, and streamline your file handling processes today!

item.content:encoded: 🧠 How We Built Our Own ZIP Handler from Scratch: Complete Technical Journey (Pagonic Project)

item.title: 🧠 How We Built Our Own ZIP Handler from Scratch: Complete Technical Journey (Pagonic Project)
item.author: SetraTheX
item.content:encoded:
A journey of building a production-ready ZIP engine from scratch with AI support, achieving 253.7 MB/s performance.🎯 Challenge: Why We Built Our Own ZIP Handler🏗️ Architecture: Building the Foundation🔧 Technical Implementation: Deep Dive🛠️ Development ChallengesIn my previous articles, I shared how I built a modern ZIP engine using AI tools and achieved spectacular performance improvements. But the real story goes deeper - it's about building our own ZIP handler from scratch instead of relying on Python's built-in zipfile module.This article tells the complete technical journey of creating - a 4220-line production-ready ZIP engine with AI-assisted optimizations, achieving 602.6 MB/s extraction speed. ZIP64 support is in development and test results for 4GB+ files will be shared when completed.
🎯 Challenge: Why We Built Our Own ZIP Handler 💡 What you'll learn in this section: Limitations of standard libraries, our vision, and why we decided to develop a custom solution.
Problems with Standard Libraries : General-purpose, limited optimization potential: 2.8 MB/s baseline performance was unacceptable: 4GB+ files couldn't be processed: AI-assisted optimizations couldn't be applied: Full control over format parsingAI-assisted optimizations: Pattern recognition and adaptive strategies: SIMD CRC32 and memory operations: 600+ MB/s target (achieved!)
🏗️ Architecture: Building the Foundation 🏗️ What you'll learn in this section: System architecture, component structure, and fundamental design decisions.zip_handler.py (4220 lines) ├── ZIP Format Parser (zip_structs.py) ├── SIMD Optimizations (simd_crc32.py) ├── Hybrid Decompressor (hybrid_decompressor.py) ├── Buffer Pool System (buffer_pool.py) ├── AI Optimization Engine (ai_optimizer.py) └── Parallel Processing (zip_parallel_orchestrator.py) : Each component <400 lines for Copilot compatibility: Fast path for small files, optimized path for large files: Proper synchronization for parallel processing: Works with existing ZIP files
🔧 Technical Implementation: Deep Dive 🔧 What you'll learn in this section: Technical implementation of each component, challenges faced, and solutions.
1. ZIP Format Parser (zip_structs.py) : Understanding and implementing ZIP file format from scratchCreated dataclass structures for all ZIP headersImplemented offset-based binary parsingAdded ZIP64 support for large filesBuilt robust error handling
2. SIMD CRC32 Optimization (simd_crc32.py) : CRC32 validation was a major bottleneckHardware-accelerated CRC32 with crc32c libraryFallback to zlib.crc32 for compatibilityAchieved 8-9x speed improvement
3. Hybrid Fast Path Strategy (hybrid_decompressor.py) : Different file sizes require different optimization strategiesSmall files (<10MB): Direct zlib decompressionLarge files (≥10MB): Buffer pools and optimized streamsAutomatic strategy selection based on file size
4. Buffer Pool System (buffer_pool.py) : Memory fragmentation and repeated allocationsPre-allocated buffer pools (64KB to 8MB)Memory pressure management
5. AI-Assisted Optimization (ai_optimizer.py) : How to automatically select optimal parameters for each filePattern recognition for 5 file typesAdaptive compression levels (1-9)Dynamic chunk sizing (64KB-4MB)
📊 Performance Results: From 2.8 to 602.6 MB/s
Current Benchmark Results Baseline (Python zipfile): 2.8 MB/s Our ZIP Handler: 602.6 MB/s (extraction) Improvement: +21,421% Compression Speed: 333.0 MB/s (peak) Extraction Speed: 602.6 MB/s (peak)
📈 Performance Comparison Chart Speed (MB/s) Baseline Our Handler 700 ┤ ╭─ 602.6 600 ┤ ╭───╯ 500 ┤ ╭───╯ 400 ┤ ╭───╯ 300 ┤ ╭───╯ 333.0 200 ┤ ╭───╯ 100 ┤ ╭───╯ 0 ┼────────────╯ Extraction Compression ┌─────────────────┬─────────────┬─────────────┐ │ Metric │ Baseline │ Ours │ ├─────────────────┼─────────────┼─────────────┤ │ Extraction Speed│ 2.8 MB/s │ 602.6 MB/s │ │ Compression │ 1.5 MB/s │ 333.0 MB/s │ │ Memory Usage │ 500 MB │ 24.5 MB │ │ Test Success │ 85% │ 100% │ └─────────────────┴─────────────┴─────────────┘ : 459.6 MB/s (average) - 602.6 MB/s (peak): 217.1 MB/s (average) - 333.0 MB/s (peak): 64 successful detections: Average 24.5 MB usage: 100% pass rate: Full support: Windows/Linux compatibility: Thread-safe and robust: 12 cores (ideal for high parallel performance): 15.93 GB total, 6.16 GB available: 464.98 GB total, 181.54 GB free: Windows 10 (powerful system): The reason for such high parallel extraction speeds is the 12-core powerful processor and sufficient RAM. These results were achieved on high-performance systems.
🤖 AI Integration: Beyond Traditional Optimization
Pattern Recognition System
Adaptive Strategy Selection : Automatic categorization: Entropy-based analysisSystem resource monitoring: Memory and CPU pressure: Learning from previous operations
🚀 Advanced Features: Parallel Processing and Future Plans : 602.6 MB/s peak performance (12-core system): Multiple files simultaneously: Thread-safe memory management: 64 successful detections: Average 24.5 MB usage: Maximum performance on 12-core systems: In development (for 4GB+ files): Extreme large files (5GB-10GB) tests planned: Remote file processing support: Advanced security and compliance
🛠️ Development Challenges and Solutions
Challenge 1: Copilot File Size Limits and AI Crashes : 4220-line zip_handler.py exceeded Copilot's scanning limits and AI started crashing continuously: "I was fed up with Copilot. Lines kept increasing and AI kept crashing. After my long planning was done, I said 'this will work' and switched to Cursor. Problem solved."Modular architecture with <400 line componentsExtracted optimizations to separate modulesImproved tool compatibility while maintaining functionality: Started using Cursor when Copilot limits were exceeded
Challenge 2: Thread Safety : Parallel processing caused race conditionsGlobal locks for folder creationThread-isolated file handlesProper exception handling
Challenge 3: Memory Management : Large files caused memory overflowMemory-mapped file support
📈 Lessons Learned: The Reality of AI-Assisted Development : ChatGPT helped design modular structure: AI was excellent at defining optimization patterns: Copilot was great for repetitive boilerplate: AI helped create comprehensive test suitesCode Example - AI Pattern Recognition:: AI struggled with complex memory management: Required manual fine-tuning: Required careful manual review: AI couldn't handle complete system integrationCode Example - Manual Thread Safety:AI is a tool, not a replacement: Manual intervention was often necessaryModular design is critical: Keeps files manageable for AI tools: Comprehensive validation of AI-generated code requiredPerformance requires iteration: Multiple optimization cycles necessaryCode Example - Modular Design:
🎯 Future Roadmap: What's Next : Full support for 4GB+ files (in development): Benchmark for 5GB-10GB extreme large files: User-friendly interface: 7z, RAR support: Remote file processing: Advanced security and compliance: Make project open source: Extensible architecture: 700+ MB/s target (above current 602.6 MB/s)
💡 Insights: Building Production Software with AI Custom implementations, when optimized for specific use cases, can exceed standard librariesModular architecture is essential for AI-assisted developmentPerformance optimization requires multiple iterations and careful measurementThread safety and error handling are critical for production systemsAI excels at pattern recognition and code generation but struggles with complex system integrationManual intervention is often necessary for performance-critical codeTesting is more important than ever when using AI-generated codeDocumentation and clear architecture help AI tools work more effectivelyCustom solutions can provide competitive advantage in performance-critical applicationsAI-assisted development can accelerate development but requires expert supervisionPerformance optimization can be a significant differentiator in software productsModular, maintainable code is essential for long-term success
🎯 Conclusion: Journey Summary 🏆 : Building our own ZIP handler from scratch was a challenging but rewarding journey.┌─────────────────────────┬─────────────────┬─────────────────┐ │ Metric │ Target │ Achieved │ ├─────────────────────────┼─────────────────┼─────────────────┤ │ Extraction Performance │ 150+ MB/s │ 602.6 MB/s │ │ Compression Performance │ 100+ MB/s │ 333.0 MB/s │ │ Test Success │ 85%+ │ 100% │ │ AI Pattern Detection │ 50+ │ 64 │ │ Memory Efficiency │ <100 MB │ 24.5 MB │ └─────────────────────────┴─────────────────┴─────────────────┘ AI-assisted development can create powerful custom solutions that exceed standard librariesCareful architecture, comprehensive testing, and expert supervision are essentialModular design is critical for AI toolsPerformance optimization requires multiple iterationsThis project shows that with the right approach, AI tools can help developers build sophisticated, high-performance software that would be difficult to create manually.: ZIP64 support is in development and test results for 4GB+ files will be shared when completed. Additionally, stress tests for 5GB-10GB extreme large files are planned.: These performance results were achieved on a 12-core powerful system. Parallel extraction speeds are specifically optimized for multi-core systems.📦 : Pagonic ZIP Engine🚀 : 602.6 MB/s extraction speed (peak, 12-core system)🤖 : Pattern recognition and adaptive optimization💻 : 12 cores, 16GB RAM, Windows 10
💬 Personal Experiences: Questions and Answers The most valuable lessons and personal experiences I learned throughout this journey:
🎯 Biggest Challenge: AI Tool Limitations: "What was the biggest challenge you faced in this project?": The biggest challenge was AI tools starting to crash as file size increased. When reached 4000+ lines, Copilot completely crashed. Every change would freeze the IDE and AI would just give up.Code Example - The Problem:: "I was fed up with Copilot. Lines kept increasing and AI kept crashing. After my long planning was done, I said 'this will work' and switched to Cursor. Problem solved."This experience taught me the practical limits of AI tools and showed the importance of modular architecture.
🧠 Technical Learning: From Naive to Systematic Development: "What was your biggest technical learning from this project?": The biggest learning was how to develop software systematically even with AI assistance. I started with a naive approach - just asking AI to build features - but quickly learned that real progress requires a structured methodology.Phase 1: Template-First Development - Learned to create standardized module templates (50% speedup)Phase 2: Copy-Paste Engineering - Learned to systematically identify and reuse proven code blocksPhase 3: Manual-AI Hybrid Approach - Learned to manually implement code with AI guidance when tools hit limitsPhase 4: Modular Architecture - Realized keeping files under 300 lines is critical for AI tool compatibilityThis approach became so systematic that I documented it in detailed planning files like .
🤖 AI Integration: Surprises and Realities: "What surprised you most about AI in the development process?": What surprised me was AI being excellent at pattern recognition and code generation but struggling with complex system integration. AI was great at defining optimization patterns but required manual intervention for complex memory management and thread safety.AI for architectural designPattern recognition and optimization strategiesBoilerplate code generationComplex memory managementPerformance-critical optimizationsComplete system integration
📊 Performance Insights: Biggest Surprise: "Which performance optimization surprised you most and why?": The impact of the buffer pooling system surprised me most. It started as a simple memory management optimization but achieved dramatic performance improvement with 100% hit rate.: Sometimes the simplest optimizations create the biggest impact. Buffer pooling improved performance through smart memory management rather than complex algorithms.
🚀 Future Plans: Next Big Challenge: "What big challenge are you planning to tackle next?": ZIP64 support and stress tests for extreme large files (5GB-10GB). ZIP64 is currently in development and I'll share test results for 4GB+ files when completed.Complete ZIP64 support (4GB+ files)5GB-10GB extreme large file stress tests700+ MB/s performance target (above current 602.6 MB/s)Cloud integration and enterprise features
😅 Funny/Frustrating Moments: Educational Experiences: "Did you have any funny or frustrating moments during development?": Yes! Copilot constantly crashing and me saying "this time it will definitely work" and trying again was funny. Every change would freeze the IDE in 4000+ line files but I still hoped "maybe this time.": When I finally decided to switch to Cursor, I restructured the entire project into modular components and the problem was solved. This taught me the lesson "accept tool limitations and adapt.": Sometimes the best solution isn't fighting with the current tool, but finding the right tool or changing approach.If you're inspired by this project, you can start your own AI-assisted development journey:Start with a small project - Simple optimizations instead of complex systems - Manageable file sizes for AI toolsWrite comprehensive tests - Validate AI-generated code correctness - Track progress with concrete metricsWould you like to share your AI-assisted development experiences too?What challenges did you face?Which AI tools did you use?I'd love to compare notes! 🚀
👨‍💻 Developer Information : SetraTheXX: Pagonic ZIP Engine: SetraTheXX: Available through GitHub: AI-assisted development, performance optimization, custom ZIP implementations: GitHub Copilot, Cursor, ChatGPT: 602.6 MB/s extraction speed (peak): Modular, thread-safe, production-ready: 112 tests, 100% pass rateZIP64 support developmentExtreme large file testing (5GB-10GB)Performance optimization to 700+ MB/sOpen source release preparationBuilt custom ZIP handler from scratchAchieved 21,421% performance improvement over baselineImplemented AI-assisted pattern recognitionCreated modular, maintainable architectureThis project demonstrates the power of AI-assisted development when combined with systematic methodology and expert supervision.

Step 2: Building Our AI Agent
Step 3: Creating Intelligent Workflows Now let's add automated workflows. Create :
Step 4: Putting It All Together Create to run the complete system:

Key Concepts We've Implemented

Agent-Server CommunicationThe agent connects to the MCP server and can:Read resources (feedback data)Call tools (collect feedback, analyze sentiment)Process results intelligentlyUnderstand natural languageExtract information from conversationsGenerate insights and recommendationsCreate personalized responsesRespond to events (negative feedback)Generate reports automatically

item.title: The Building Blocks - Understanding MCP, Agents, and LLMs (Part 2/5)
item.author: bredmond1019
item.content:encoded:
In Part 1, we learned that AI systems are like smart orchestra conductors. Now, let's meet the musicians in our orchestra and understand how they work together. By the end of this article, you'll understand the core components we'll use to build our AI system.
The Four Essential Components
1. Workflow Engines: The ConductorA workflow engine orchestrates the entire process. Think of it as a recipe that says:First, collect the customer feedbackThen, analyze the sentimentNext, categorize the issuesFinally, generate a summary reportPopular workflow engines include:: Visual workflow automation: Code-based workflows: Data pipeline orchestration
2. AI Agents: The Specialized WorkersAI agents are like skilled employees, each with a specific job:: Gathers information from various sources: Processes and understands data: Creates reports and summaries: Performs tasks like sending emailsHere's a simple conceptual example:
3. Large Language Models: The BrainsLLMs are the intelligence behind AI agents. They:Understand natural languageGenerate human-like responsesAnalyze patterns and sentimentMake contextual decisions: Powerful and versatile: Great for analysis and coding: Privacy-focused alternatives
4. MCP Servers: The Universal ConnectorsHere's where things get exciting. Model Context Protocol (MCP) is like a universal adapter that lets AI systems connect to any tool or service.A filing cabinet (your database)A telephone (communication system)A calculator (processing tool)MCP creates a standard way for AI to use all these tools, regardless of their original design.
How MCP Works: A Simple Analogy Think of MCP like a restaurant menu:"Today's Specials" (dynamic data)"Regular Menu" (static information) = Things You Can Order"Get me the soup of the day""I'll have the daily special""Can you modify this dish?"Communicates with the kitchen
Why MCP Changes Everything Before MCP, connecting AI to different tools was like needing a different translator for each country you visit. With MCP, it's like everyone suddenly speaks the same language.: One protocol for all integrations: Easy to add new capabilities: Clear boundaries and permissions: Less code, more functionality
A Sneak Peek: Our MCP Server Structure Here's what we'll build in the upcoming parts:When these components work together: says: "Time to analyze today's feedback" receives the task and thinks: "I need to get the feedback first" provides access to the feedback data analyzes the sentiment and extracts insights flow back through the system to generate reportsIn Part 3, we'll roll up our sleeves and set up our development environment. You'll:Install Python and the MCP SDKSet up your first MCP serverPrepare for building our feedback analysis systemThe exciting part? Once you understand these building blocks, you can apply them to ANY business problem.What tools or services would you want to connect to an AI system? Drop a comment below - your idea might become our next example!

item.title: Building a Real-Time Crypto Pipeline with Binance APIs, PostgreSQL, Debezium, Kafka, Spark & Cassandra
item.author: Denzel Kanyeki
item.content:encoded:
In this blog post, I’ll walk you through a real-time crypto data pipeline I built using a powerful stack: for structured storage and a data source 🔹 Debezium + Confluent Kafka for Change Data Capture (CDC) for stream processing for fast NoSQL storage hosted on an Azure Ubuntu virtual Machine used as a data sink.This architecture enables near real-time data extraction and transformation, ideal for live dashboards, analytics, distributed systems, and downstream uses e.g., building of machine learning models.This project is divided into four phases:Extracting data from the Binance API endpoints, transforming and loading the data into a PostgreSQL database using Python scripts.Using a Debezium Source connector to connect into the PostgreSQL database to a Kafka topic to store change logsUsing a Spark Streaming job to act as a sink to send our replicated data from the Kafka topics into a Cassandra keyspace Visualizing the data in Apache Cassandra on a Grafana dashboard using a PostgreSQL bridge to pull data from Cassandra.For more technical details on the project, check the linked GitHub repository.
What is Change Data Capture (CDC)? Change Data Capture (CDC) is a technique used in data pipelines to identify and track changes made to data in a database. It captures these changes, , , and , and makes them available for use in other systems, often in real-time or near real-time. CDC helps improve data efficiency and consistency across systems by only replicating the changed data, rather than the entire dataset. There are three types of CDC mechanisms namely:: This captures changes by reading the database’s transaction logs, which are used internally to ensure durability and consistency. For example, PostgreSQL uses Write-Ahead Logs (WALs) to record all changes before they're applied to the actual database. Tools like Debezium tap into these logs using logical decoding and convert them into structured JSON messages. These include:
: The state of the record before the change.: The new state after the change.: Metadata about the origin of the change e.g., database, table, LSN, timestamp.An example of a log captured from Postgres into a Kafka Topic via a Debezium connector in JSON:: This method involves periodically querying the source database to identify changes, usually by comparing the last updated timestamps or using fields. Below is an example: : This method uses database triggers to detect changes and write them to an audit/log table. An example includes: would write the old and new row values to a separate table which will store the changes.This project requires the following:Debezium's PostgresCDCConnectorV2 connector, available on Confluent CloudMicrosoft Azure Virtual Machine to host our Apache Cassandra. (You can host it locally if you want to)The Python script extracts and transforms 5 types of data from the Binance REST API endpoints:30-minute Klines / CandlesticksHere's an example of the extraction, transformation and loading of the prices data:After transformation, it loads the data into a PostgreSQL database's schema. The PostgreSQL tables created are:Head over to Confluent Cloud and if you do not have an account, please create a free account to claim free $400 credits and create an environment and cluster. When prompted to add a payment method.After creating a cluster, on the left-hand side, select and search for Postgres CDC Source V2 (Debezium) connector and start the setup.Here's the connector receiving data from the Postgres database and storing it into Kafka topics.A script containing a Spark Streaming job that acts as a data sink into Apache Cassandra.It consumes the logs from the various Kafka topics into JSON format, flattens after, source, op, ts_ms, and transaction fields, transforms them, and loads them into Cassandra hosted on an Azure Ubuntu VMThe data received in Cassandra is as follows for the table:After streaming data into Cassandra, we need to visualize this data on a Grafana dashboard to derive insights from the data.For this project, I have used Grafana Cloud. There is a plugin that is used as a data source, but it only supports Cassandra 3.0.0 and older, and I am running 4.1.9 hence coming into some issues with connecting GrafanaHence, I built a simple Postgres-Cassandra bridge to send data to a PostgreSQL database which Grafana natively supports to draw insights from the data.NOTE: By default, Cassandra comes with an authenticator and authorizer set to AllowAllAuthenticator, which disables authentication and allows any user to access Cassandra's shell . Remove the default configuration, and restart the Cassandra service as so:nano /etc/cassandra/cassandra.yaml Then exit the editor and run the following comand to restart the Cassandra service:systemctl restart cassandra
Insights gained from the data The dashboards reveal several valuable insights into crypto trading patterns. The first chart shows that XRPUSDT, AVAXUSDT, BTCUSDT, and DOGEUSDT are among the most frequently traded symbols in the trading day, indicating high liquidity or market interest. The ETHUSDT volume trend indicates significant fluctuations, with notable spikes between 13:00 and 14:00, suggesting peak trading hours possibly influenced by market news or institutional actions. The candlestick chart for BTCUSDT displays hourly open, high, low, and close prices, offering a useful tool for technical analysis and identifying market trends or reversal patterns.Additionally, the quantity of coins ordered throughout the trading day highlights DOGEUSDT and ADAUSDT as dominant in trade volume, with DOGE exceeding 270,000 units, likely due to its lower price and retail trader appeal. Overall, the visualizations suggest strong market activity around a few key coins, with volume and price data providing critical insights for real-time monitoring, trading strategy, or machine learning applications.This project was a rewarding journey through the world of real-time data engineering. It demonstrates how Change Data Capture can save time and resources while updating our databases for streaming applications and data pipelines. This stack can easily be used for downstream use cases e.g. data analytics or even real-time ML predictions for crypto coins.Do you have any questions? Don't forget to like, comment and follow for more data engineering tutorials and content!

ScanMeee is more than just a QR code generator; it is an entertainment-focused tool that turns the mundane task of creating QR codes into a fun experience. Whether you're designing a marketing campaign, setting up an event, or simply want to share a link in a unique way, ScanMeee provides the creative spark you need. The service is easy to use and offers a variety of designs and themes to suit different purposes.With ScanMeee, you can easily create QR codes that not only serve their primary function—linking to a URL or information—but also bring a smile to users' faces. The platform makes the process engaging by allowing you to customize your codes with various styles, shapes, and colors. ScanMeee simplifies the sharing process by providing easy-to-use links and options to share your QR codes directly on social media platforms. As a developer, you understand the importance of integrating social sharing into your applications. ScanMeee supports developers in creating engaging content that can drive interactions and expand reach.What sets ScanMeee apart is its commitment to entertainment. QR codes can often feel like just another task on our to-do list. ScanMeee transforms this perception by injecting fun into the experience, making it enjoyable not only for developers but also for end-users. This focus on entertainment can help create a more engaging user experience across your applications.
Why Developers Should Care As a developer, incorporating features that enhance user engagement is crucial. By utilizing a tool like ScanMeee, you can enrich your applications with interactive elements that captivate users. Whether you are developing for marketing, events, or community engagement, having a unique QR code can set your project apart from the competition.Moreover, the ease of integration allows you to focus on what you do best—building great software—while providing a delightful experience for your users.
Get Started with ScanMeee Ready to add a fun twist to your QR code creation? Visit ScanMeee to explore the platform and start generating entertaining QR codes that align with your project's goals. Incorporate creativity into your development workflow and let your QR codes do more than just link; let them entertain. Don’t miss out on the opportunity to engage with your audience in a fresh, dynamic way. Check out ScanMeee today and see the difference it can make in your project!

item.title: 8 Advanced Python Serialization Techniques for High-Performance Data Processing
item.author: Aarav Joshi
item.content:encoded:
As a best-selling author, I invite you to explore my books on Amazon. Don't forget to follow me on Medium and show your support. Thank you! Your support means the world! Data serialization transforms Python objects into portable formats for storage or transmission. Deserialization reverses this process. Both operations are fundamental in modern applications. I've found that choosing the right approach significantly impacts performance and reliability. Below are eight practical techniques I regularly use to optimize these processes.Binary serialization with MessagePack offers exceptional efficiency. When working with IoT sensor networks, I noticed JSON payloads consumed excessive bandwidth. MessagePack's binary format reduced our data size by nearly half. The implementation remains straightforward. Consider this sensor data example:Schema-based serialization with Protocol Buffers ensures strict data validation. In a recent microservices project, this prevented versioning errors between teams. We define a .proto file:Compile it with protoc --python_out=. device_alert.proto, then use in Python:Custom JSON encoders handle special objects seamlessly. When our analytics platform needed to serialize datetime and NumPy arrays, we implemented this:For deserialization, add a corresponding decoder:Compression techniques like LZ4 dramatically reduce network load. During a distributed computing project, we accelerated data transfers by 40%:Partial deserialization with ijson avoids loading entire files. When analyzing multi-gigabyte JSON logs, I use:Streaming serialization manages memory constraints. For database exports exceeding RAM, I implement chunked processing:Security hardening prevents deserialization attacks. After a security audit revealed vulnerabilities, we implemented payload signing:Parallel processing accelerates batch operations. For transforming terabytes of legacy data, we used:
These techniques address different aspects of data handling. MessagePack provides compact binary serialization. Protocol Buffers enforce structure. Custom JSON encoders extend flexibility. Compression reduces size. Partial parsing handles large files. Streaming conserves memory. Security features protect integrity. Parallelism improves throughput. Selecting the right combination depends on your specific requirements for speed, safety, and compatibility. In my experience, layering these approaches—like compressing schema-based serializations—yields robust solutions. 📘 , , , and to the channel! is an AI-driven publishing company co-founded by author . By leveraging advanced AI technology, we keep our publishing costs incredibly low—some books are priced as low as —making quality knowledge accessible to everyone.Stay tuned for updates and exciting news. item.title: Apache Kafka: How-to set offsets to a fixed time
item.author: Nucu Labs
item.content:encoded: This is a short article about setting offsets in Apache Kafka for a consumer group.Normally, to reset offsets in Kafka you need to use the kafka-consumer-groups.sh tool, this means downloading the zip archive with Kafka's source code and setting up the Java SDK. All Kafka's tools are dependent on Java and this isn't that nice or developer friendly...Sometimes getting Java correctly and getting the tools to run they don't work 🤷🏻‍♂️. Either the tool versions are incompatible with the Kafka version on the server or the command executes successfully but it doesn't seem to do anything...Another method to set offsets for a consumer it is to use a Kafka library, and to do it through code.I have Python installed on my setup and all I need to do is to install the confluent-kafka library:pip install confluent-kafka And then run the following code snippet to reset the consumer's offsets to a specific timestamp:

item.title: 📊 Time Series Analysis: A Practical Guide for Data Scientists
item.author: Abhishek Jaiswal
item.content:encoded:
“Time is what we want most, but what we use worst.” — William PennIn the world of data, is more than just a timestamp—it's a pattern, a signal, and sometimes even a story waiting to be told. Welcome to the world of —a powerful tool for forecasting, detecting anomalies, and uncovering seasonality in data that evolves over time.Whether you're a beginner in data science or a professional building predictive systems, understanding time series analysis is a must-have skill in your toolkit.
📌 What is Time Series Data? In simple terms, is a sequence of data points collected or recorded at successive points in time, usually at equally spaced intervals (daily, hourly, monthly, etc.).Electricity consumption ⚡Retail sales across months 🛒What makes time series unique is the — the idea that past values influence future values.
⚙️ Key Components of Time Series Before jumping into modeling, it's crucial to break a time series down into its key components:A long-term increase or decrease in the data.Example: Sales increasing steadily over years due to business expansion.Patterns that repeat at regular intervals (daily, weekly, yearly).Example: Ice cream sales peaking every summer.Longer-term fluctuations without a fixed frequency.Example: Economic recessions.Random variations or fluctuations in the data that cannot be explained.
🔍 Why is Time Series Analysis Important? Time series analysis goes beyond simple observation. It enables:✅ Forecasting future values ✅ (detecting spikes or drops) ✅ (inventory, marketing, risk) ✅ (trend, seasonality)From to , time series is everywhere.
🧠 Techniques in Time Series Analysis A simple technique that smooths data by averaging values over a defined window.Gives more weight to recent observations—great for short-term forecasting.
3. ARIMA (AutoRegressive Integrated Moving Average)A statistical model that captures autocorrelation, trends, and seasonality.
4. An extension of ARIMA that supports seasonality.An open-source model from Meta that handles trend changepoints and seasonality easily. Beginner-friendly and interpretable.
6. LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory)A type of recurrent neural network used for capturing long-term dependencies. Excellent for complex datasets like electricity usage, traffic, etc.
🧪 Real-World Applications of Time Series Patient vital signs monitoringForecasting temperature, rainfall, etc.
📊 Time Series Forecasting: Step-by-Step Here’s a typical workflow for any time series project:Start with a time-indexed dataset. Ensure timestamps are regular.
2. Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)Visualize trends, seasonality, and outliers using:Rolling window statisticsTime-based features (weekday, month)Use ARIMA, Prophet, or LSTM depending on complexity and interpretability.RMSE (Root Mean Squared Error)MAE (Mean Absolute Error)MAPE (Mean Absolute Percentage Error)
7. Generate future predictions and integrate them into business pipelines.
⚠️ Common Pitfalls in Time Series 🚫 Ignoring autocorrelation 🚫 Using standard ML models without feature engineering 🚫 Failing to account for seasonality 🚫 Training on future data (data leakage!)
🔮 Time Series in 2025 and Beyond With advancements in deep learning and the rise of and , we're entering an exciting era.Temporal Fusion Transformers…are redefining what's possible in forecasting.
🛠️ Tools & Libraries You’ll Love ✅ — Time-based indexing and manipulation ✅ — ARIMA and decomposition ✅ — Quick and interpretable forecasts ✅ — Feature engineering + ML models ✅ / — LSTM, RNN models ✅ , , — Advanced forecasting librariesTime series analysis isn't just about predicting numbers—it's about understanding how things change with time, and using that knowledge to drive decisions.Whether you're trying to reduce stockouts, prepare for demand spikes, or anticipate system failures—time is your most valuable variable.So next time you see a dataset with timestamps—don’t just throw it into a linear regressor. Listen to what time is trying to tell you.

item.content:encoded:
In today's rapidly evolving tech landscape, it's easy for developers to get caught up in the latest frameworks, languages, and trends. But what if you could leverage your skills to make a positive impact on the world? Enter Helpothon, a platform dedicated to harnessing technology for social good while building vibrant communities that foster collaboration and innovation.Helpothon is more than just a platform; it's a movement that connects developers with opportunities to create meaningful solutions for businesses and communities alike. It serves as a bridge between those who want to utilize technology to spur social change and the resources available to make it happen. With a clear focus on technology for social good, Helpothon provides developers with the tools, resources, and support they need to tackle some of the most pressing issues facing our society today.
Key Features of Helpothon
1. Technology for Social Good At its core, Helpothon is about using technology to drive positive change. Whether it's through developing applications that address social issues, creating platforms that facilitate donations, or building tools that promote sustainability, Helpothon empowers developers to use their coding skills for a greater purpose. The platform offers a variety of projects that you can contribute to, allowing you to align your passions with meaningful work. By collaborating with NGOs, non-profits, and community organizations, you can directly impact lives and communities through technology.Helpothon thrives on collaboration and community.
